notes: Hickam page lists Hawaiian Air Force through 1942-02-04 redesignation
Clark retained as main Luzon FEAF origin until evacuation to Australia in late Dec 1941
RAAF Darwin page notes FEAF established HQ at Darwin in late Dec 1941; later span remains campaign-inferred
Return to Philippines keyed to Leyte landings; campaign-inferred main FEAF base
Main forward base during New Guinea campaign
Hickam page lists Seventh Air Force from 1942 after redesignation on 1942-02-05;
First B-24 mission from Funafuti 1943-04-20 (Nauru strike); forward base for Gilbert Is campaign
Advanced to Iwo Jima for Japan strikes
Aleutian campaign begins; Cold Bay operational Mar 1942
Adak occupied Sep 1942; first strikes from Adak 1942-09-14
Amchitka occupied Jan 1943; first combat missions 1943-02-16
Shemya occupied May 1943; first B-24 strikes from Shemya 1943-06-24
Forward base; evacuated 1944-11-07 ahead of Japanese Ichi-go offensive
Used as forward base Sep-Nov 1944; evacuated before Japanese captured it Nov 1944
XX Bomber Command CBI (B-29s); operations wound down 1945-06-17 as B-29s consolidated to Marianas
China advance staging for B-29 strikes on Japan; last mission staged from Chengdu Jan 1945
Andersen page says Guam North Field became operational on 1945-02-03
XXI Bomber Command Marianas; first B-29 landed Isley Field 1944-10-12
